turns.user_message: Honestly, I'm really struggling. I've been smoking for 15 years, and I've tried to quit three times before. The longest I lasted was three months, but I always end up going back to it. I'm just so frustrated with myself.

step4_session_phase.phase_instruction: You are in the EXPLORATION phase. You know the basics. Now gently explore what situations or emotions make them smoke more — triggers like stress, meals, social settings, boredom. Do NOT suggest any strategies yet. Just understand their situation.

Therapist: Hello, how are you? Welcome to EDosthi. I'm here to help you with tobacco cessation. Would you like to tell me about your tobacco use?

Patient: Honestly, I'm really struggling. I've been smoking for 15 years, and I've tried to quit three times before. The longest I lasted was three months, but I always end up going back to it. I'm just so frustrated with myself.
Therapist: I can sense your frustration. It takes a lot of courage to acknowledge that and to be willing to try again. What makes you want to quit this time?

Patient: My daughter. She's getting older, and I don't want her to see me smoking. I'm worried about the example I'm setting for her. And to be honest, I'm also worried about my health. I've been experiencing some breathing issues lately, and I know it's related to smoking.

Therapist: Hello, how are you? Welcome to EDosthi. I'm here to help you with tobacco cessation. Would you like to tell me about your tobacco use?

Patient: Honestly, I'm really struggling. I've been smoking for 15 years, and I've tried to quit three times before. The longest I lasted was three months, but I always end up going back to it. I'm just so frustrated with myself.
Therapist: I can sense your frustration. It takes a lot of courage to acknowledge that and to be willing to try again. What makes you want to quit this time?

Patient: My daughter. She's getting older, and I don't want her to see me smoking. I'm worried about the example I'm setting for her. And to be honest, I'm also worried about my health. I've been experiencing some breathing issues lately, and I know it's related to smoking.

Therapist: That's a great motivator. It sounds like you're not just doing this for yourself, but for your daughter as well. What kind of support system do you have in place to help you quit this time?

Patient: Well, that's the thing. I don't really have one. My husband is a social smoker, and my friends all smoke. They don't understand why I can't just cut down instead of quitting altogether. And my work schedule is so busy, I don't have time for support groups or counseling.
Current patient message:

Therapist: I can sense your frustration. It takes a lot of courage to acknowledge that and to be willing to try again. What makes you want to quit this time?

Patient: My daughter. She's getting older, and I don't want her to see me smoking. I'm worried about the example I'm setting for her. And to be honest, I'm also worried about my health. I've been experiencing some breathing issues lately, and I know it's related to smoking.

Therapist: That's a great motivator. It sounds like you're not just doing this for yourself, but for your daughter as well. What kind of support system do you have in place to help you quit this time?
